the well water's been tasting like copper for a week
someone's been helping themselves to the storehouse at night
the path to the lower fields is washing out again
the roof on the meeting hall has been leaking since the last storm and no one's fixed it
rats in the grain cellar, worse than last year by a good margin
whoever's letting their livestock wander at night needs to pen them properly
the nightwatch has been falling asleep on duty, and everyone knows it
firewood keeps disappearing from the communal pile faster than it should
the fence along the eastern boundary has been down for a month
the potter and the smith won't stop arguing over the shared workspace
bread from the last batch of flour tasted off, like something had gotten into it
someone moved the boundary stones again, same dispute as always
the drainage ditch is clogged and the lower road floods every time it rains
children playing near the old foundation found loose stones and nearly got hurt
the communal oven hasn't drawn properly since the chimney cracked
a family upstream has been dumping wash water where it flows right past the houses
the bridge planks are rotting through and nobody wants to pay for new timber
smoke from the charcoal burner drifts into the village when the wind turns
someone's dog has been killing chickens again and the owner won't take responsibility
the market stalls are getting shabbier every season, and the ground turns to mud
lantern oil has been running out faster than it should, like someone's skimming the supply
the old couple on the hill road need help with their collapsed wall but they're too proud to ask
noise from the tavern goes on well past midnight now that the innkeeper stopped caring
the healer's garden got trampled and she's blaming the children, but it looked deliberate
half the tools in the shared shed have gone dull and nobody maintains them
